ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301339 covers rosé wine as a category; no Sangiovese-specific or Toscana IGT-specific term exists in FoodOn. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LQ covers rosé wine generically. No Sangiovese varietal or Toscana IGT designation exists at a more granular level in FoodEx2. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 35748005 covers wine as a substance. No rosé-specific or Sangiovese-specific SNOMED CT code exists. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard restaurant serving of rosé wine is approximately 150 ml (one glass), equivalent to roughly 150 g given wine's near-water density.

nutrient_source.title: Wine, table, rosé — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174837)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for rosé table wine used as the closest approved match for Sangiovese Rosé Toscana IGT. Sangiovese-specific varietal data are not available in any approved primary source. Values represent a dry Italian-style rosé wine and are consistent with the product description. Alcohol content estimated at approximately 12% ABV, consistent with Toscana IGT rosé wines.

gi_source.title: Alcoholic beverages and glycemic index — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Rosé wine contains minimal fermentable carbohydrates (approximately 2.6 g per 100 g, predominantly residual sugars). Dry wines are not assigned a formal GI value in the University of Sydney GI Database because their carbohydrate content is too low to elicit a meaningful glycemic response. glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel) per Project Veritas convention for beverages with effectively zero glycemic carbohydrates. gi_category is 'low'. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 given the negligible carbohydrate contribution per 150 ml serving (approximately 3.9 g carbohydrate, yielding GL < 1).

hypertension.guidance: Regular alcohol consumption raises blood pressure; individuals with hypertension should limit rosé wine to no more than one standard drink per day and monitor blood pressure response, as even moderate intake can elevate systolic pressure by 2–4 mmHg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Alcohol-induced hypoglycaemia risk and residual sugar content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Alcohol in rosé wine can mask hypoglycaemia symptoms and potentiate insulin or sulfonylurea action; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume no more than one standard drink with food and monitor blood glucose closely, noting that the low residual sugar (approximately 1 g per 100 ml) has minimal direct glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Alcohol caloric density and arrhythmia risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While light wine consumption has been associated with modest cardioprotective effects in observational studies, current clinical guidelines do not recommend initiating alcohol intake for cardiovascular benefit; individuals with established cardiovascular disease should limit rosé wine to occasional moderate intake (≤1 standard drink/day) and avoid consumption if arrhythmia or cardiomyopathy is present.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sangiovese Rosé Toscana IGT is an Italian rosé wine produced from Sangiovese grapes grown in Tuscany, Italy, under the Indicazione Geografica Tipica (IGT) designation, which permits greater winemaking flexibility than DOC or DOCG classifications. The wine is made by brief skin contact (short maceration) with Sangiovese grapes, yielding a bright cherry-pink colour with aromas of rose petals, raspberries, citrus fruit, and subtle marine notes. On the palate it is fresh, crunchy, and delicate with a vinous, fleshy mouthfeel and a clean finish. Per 100 ml, rosé wine provides approximately 83 kcal, predominantly from alcohol (ethanol approximately 12% ABV), with minimal carbohydrate (2.6 g) and negligible protein and fat. The glycemic index is effectively zero due to the low residual sugar content, making it a low-GI beverage. Clinically, the primary concern is alcohol content: regular consumption raises blood pressure, can mask hypoglycaemia in individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, and contributes to excess caloric intake relevant to cardiovascular risk management. Sulphites are present as a preservative and represent the main allergen. This wine is not halal and is not suitable for individuals who abstain from alcohol. It is served chilled as an aperitif or paired with light Italian dishes such as antipasti, seafood, and pasta. In Singapore, it is available at Italian restaurants such as Ricciotti at The Riverwalk, priced at approximately SGD 81.75 per bottle.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy rosé table wine (FDC ID 174837), the closest approved source available. No Sangiovese-specific or Toscana IGT-specific entry exists in any approved primary source. Values are consistent with the product description and typical dry Italian rosé wine composition. Confidence is medium rather than high because the exact Vecchia Cantina product was not found in an approved database.
notes: 1. fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for rosé table wine; wine contains no fat by composition (naturally zero). 2. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are 0.0: naturally zero as wine contains no lipid fraction. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero as wine is a liquid fermented beverage with no fibre. 4. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero as wine is a plant-derived fermented beverage. 5. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for rosé wine. 6. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. 7. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. 8. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. 9. vitamin_e_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. 10. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no meaningful glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per Project Veritas convention for near-zero GI beverages. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate per 150 ml serving (~3.9 g) yields GL < 1, rounded to 0.0. 11. Input food_group was 'Grains and staples' which is incorrect for a wine; corrected to 'Beverages'. 12. Multilingual local_names are null as this is an Italian wine product, not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish; no mandatory local-language names apply under Project Veritas v2 multilingual rules. 13. is_vegan set to false as many wines use animal-derived fining agents (e.g. casein, egg albumin, isinglass); Vecchia Cantina fining practice not confirmed from approved sources. 14. Serving size 150 ml used as standard restaurant glass pour.

Added full nutrient profile from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 174837 (rosé table wine).
Documented all zero macro and micronutrient values with justification phrases in notes.
Set glycemic_index to 1 (sentinel for near-zero GI beverages) with gi_category 'low' and glycemic_load_per_serving 0.0.
Populated ontology with FoodOn, FoodEx2, and SNOMED CT codes using only 'closest_parent' match_type with gap notes.
Populated health_context for Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and Cardiovascular Disease with specific clinical guidance.
